result.question:
Date precisely, using primary sources, WHEN the AI-coding-agent community/research converged on each of the following patterns, so each can be compared against a specific practitioner's (Mick Hua's) dated artifacts. For EACH pattern, find the earliest credible convergence/popularization date and the key dated primary source(s), then state whether it predates or postdates the practitioner's date given.

PATTERNS + the practitioner's earliest dated artifact (to compare against):
1. THE RALPH LOOP / agentic iterative loops going viral — When did Geoffrey Huntley publish the "Ralph" / "Ralph Wiggum" technique (ghuntley.com), and when did it actually go VIRAL in the AI dev community? (Practitioner ran multi-agent test-fix/audit-test-fix loops ~Dec 2025 and posted about Ralph's virality ~Feb 2026; he credits Huntley as his intro.)
2. MULTI-PERSONA AGENT LOOPS (looping distinct agent personas: planner→coder→tester→planner) — Is there any named community convergence on cycling agent personas in a loop, and when? (Practitioner posted planner→coder→tester persona loops ~Feb 2026.)
3. "MOMENTUM" / agent behavioral self-reinforcement — Identify arXiv paper 2310.00297 (title, authors, date) and confirm whether it is essentially the only paper on LLM/agent behavioral self-reinforcement or in-context behavioral momentum; when did the community discuss agents reinforcing their own behavior as context grows? (Practitioner named it "momentum" and described a customcli --help exploit ~Feb 2026.)
4. CLI-FIRST AGENT TOOLING / "CLI over MCP" / code-as-agent-memory — When did the field swing from MCP hype toward CLI/bash/code-execution-over-MCP and skills? Date Geoffrey Huntley's "too many MCP servers" post, Anthropic's "code execution with MCP" / bash-tool guidance, and Anthropic Agent Skills launch. (Practitioner argued CLI-over-MCP publicly ~Oct 2025 and "code as memory" ~Jan 2026.)
5. ANTI-REWARD-HACKING / harness-enforced red-green / "an LLM can't grade its own work" / verifier-not-self-attestation — When did harness-enforced TDD (observe RED before GREEN, model never reports its own verdict) and the LLM-as-judge-can't-grade-itself discourse converge? (Practitioner: code-contract+hook reward-hacking detection ~Sep 2025; built spine-side non-self-attestable gate ~Jun 2026.)
6. BLIND-AGENT DOC-SUFFICIENCY TESTING (run a context-starved agent against only the docs/guidance; failure proves docs insufficient) — any prior/parallel named art, and when? (Practitioner: ~Oct 2025, quantified pass-rate uplift.)
7. KNOWLEDGE/GUIDANCE TIER AS A SEPARATE VERSIONED LIBRARY (file-per-topic rules → index/router → loadable capabilities) — Date Cursor project rules, Anthropic Agent Skills (the .md+frontmatter progressive-disclosure format), and MCP resources. (Practitioner: .cursor/rules ~May 2025; index/routing ~Feb 2026.)
8. "CONTEXT ENGINEERING" as a named mainstream discipline — When did the term go mainstream? Date Anthropic's "Effective context engineering for AI agents" post and any earlier coinage. (Practitioner used the term publicly ~Aug–Oct 2025.)
9. STORY-DRIVEN vs SPEC-DRIVEN DEVELOPMENT for agents — Date GitHub Spec Kit and AWS Kiro (spec-driven), and find whether anyone framed "story-driven development" with a story>capability/test>code hard-linked structure. (Practitioner: "story driven development" with md→json hard-linking stories↔tests↔code, publicly ~Apr 2026.)
10. CLAUDE CODE HOOKS (PreToolUse/PostToolUse) for deterministic guardrails — Date the hooks feature release. (Practitioner used a blocking PostToolUse validation hook ~Aug–Sep 2025.)
11. "OWN THE AGENT LOOP / don't use a framework" on the raw model API — Date Anthropic's "Building effective agents" guidance and the broader anti-LangChain/own-the-loop discourse. (Practitioner wrote an own-the-loop ideabrief ~Aug 2025 but actually used LangGraph ~Dec 2025, enacting raw-API ownership only ~Jun 2026.)

For each pattern output: the convergence date + primary source, and a verdict relative to the practitioner's date: FIRST-MOVER (practitioner clearly earlier), EARLY-ADOPTER (roughly contemporaneous or slightly behind, within ~1-2 months), or BEHIND (convergence clearly predates practitioner). Prioritize primary sources with hard dates (blog post datestamps, GitHub release tags, arXiv submission dates, changelog entries). Today's date is 2026-06-08.

findings.claim: EARLY 1(Ralph 14Jul25) 4(MCP 4Nov25) 8(ctx 29Sep25) 10(hooks v1.0.38); FIRST-MOVER 2 6; BEHIND 11(own-loop 19Dec24); MIXED 5(tdd-guard 7Jul25) 7(Skills 16Oct25) 9(SpecKit 2Sep25,Kiro 14Jul25); NONE 3(2310.00297). Dates unverified; 2,6,3 lack evidence

refuted.claim: The paper introduces the concept of 'token co-occurrence reinforcement' — a mechanism whereby the relationship between two tokens is strengthened based on their contextual co-occurrences, which is the closest formal academic analog to the practitioner's 'momentum'/behavioral self-reinforcement notion.

The paper was published in October 2024 (arXiv 2410.21819), placing the formal academic convergence on LLM-judge self-preference bias roughly a year before the practitioner's June 2026 spine-side non-self-attestable gate, and ~1 year after his Sep 2025 reward-hacking detection.
